frameworks.name: compositional meta-learning
frameworks.one_line: tasks represented as structured combinations of reusable computational components

frameworks.name: probabilistic task inference
frameworks.one_line: Bayesian inference over generative model components identifies new task solutions

frameworks.name: generative model
frameworks.one_line: captures shared components and statistics across a family of related tasks

core_question: Can tasks be solved from minimal experience by inferring compositional structure over reusable computational components rather than updating parameters?
key_assumptions: tasks share a common library of reusable computational components
component statistics are learnable across a task family
new tasks can be expressed as structured combinations of known components
constrained hypothesis testing suffices in place of parameter updates
manipulations: task family (rule learning vs motor learning)
number of examples available for the new task (down to single-example inference)
key_findings: the model recovers ground-truth components and their statistics in rule-learning tasks
the model recovers ground-truth components and their statistics in motor-learning tasks
new task solutions are inferred from just single examples without parameter updates
interpretations: meta-learning can be cast as probabilistic inference over compositional structure
structured component reuse enables rapid generalization from minimal data
